fields.hero.title: A whole game console that fits on your fingertip.
fields.hero.sub: smol turns a $3 ESP32-C3 and a tiny 0.42″ OLED into a pocket handheld — driven by a Bluetooth Stadia controller, running a Minecraft-ish block digger.
fields.vitals.intro: Every number below was pulled from the chip over USB with esptool — not a datasheet guess. This is exactly what we're working with.
fields.game.desc: Gravity pulls you down. Walk the surface, dig blocks into your inventory, then place them back to build. It's Minecraft distilled to its purest loop and squeezed onto 2,880 pixels — the whole world fits in a screen the size of a postage stamp.
fields.game.features:
• Procedural world: sky, grass, dirt, stone
  • Real gravity + auto step-up walking
  • Dig / place with a live inventory counter
  • Renders to a 1-bit framebuffer at ~60 fps
fields.game.why: Bedrock needs ~2–4 GB of RAM and a GPU. smol has 400 KB and a 1-bit screen — off by roughly 10,000×. So instead of faking it, Block Digger keeps the soul of Minecraft (dig, gather, build) at a scale the hardware loves. The mockup on the left is the actual render logic, running in your browser.
fields.controls.desc: The board has just two buttons — one of them is RESET — so a real controller matters. A Stadia controller flashed to Bluetooth mode is a standard BLE gamepad, and the C3 is BLE-only, so it's a perfect match. The Bluepad32 library handles pairing; the game reads the D-pad and face buttons.
fields.mission.intro: This board updates itself. Tasks and the agents working them are tracked in real time as smol comes together — flashing firmware, wrangling git, and more. The full-auto team has landed, on real glass across all three boards (build 45 “Oxidized Die”): a shared-world MMO snake with six magical treasure-powers, a 3-way ESP-NOW mesh with newest-wins time sync, realm-sigil board names, a plugin framework (About + scrolling menu + Bench mesh-view), the internet relay proven end-to-end, and an MQTT-native Home Assistant link — every board mirrors live battery, state-of-charge and grid readings on its OLED, hardware-verified on the fleet. In progress now: a Home Assistant node manager (set each board’s screen remotely), a no-burst roaming mesh that keeps the radio awake during WiFi sync (coexistence proven in a hardware soak), and over-the-air updates (toolchain proven and designed, rolling out canary-first). Every wave closes through adversarial review passes capped by a byte-exact gate.
fields.roadmap.intro: Ideas we're kicking around. Edit this list as the project grows — it's your design doc.
fields.roadmap.list:
• WORLD SNAKEA shared 256×256 world every board’s snake roams at once — scrolling viewport, no walls, peers by their magical name, a mesh leaderboard. Committed + FLASHED and running on all three boards (waves 3+4); 2-board head-to-head verified, live N-board play is the last check. 18 B frames @5 Hz. Issue #5.
  • TREASURE POWERSSix collectible magical powers for World Snake — phase, haste, shield, golden growth, reveal, rebirth. Deterministic rare spawns; all in code + committed (52/52), flashed + running on all three boards.
  • MESH TIME SYNCBoards agree on the time over ESP-NOW — newest NTP sync wins, loop-free. Flashed; a 3-way mesh verified across all three boards.
  • MAGICAL NAMESEvery board earns a realm-sigil fantasy name shown on-screen — id7 “Draconic Dominion”, id8 “Eldritch Nexus”, id9 “Jade Herald”. Flashed on all three boards.
  • INTERNET RELAYAn in-range board bridges a far peer’s telemetry out: ESP-NOW → WiFi burst → Home Assistant over MQTT. E2E VERIFIED + sustained on hardware — leaf→gateway→WiFi→HA, still flowing across a firmware upgrade. One follow-up: a cold-ARP first-round retry. Issue #3.
  • HOME-ASSISTANT LINKEvery board shows live Home Assistant data on its OLED — battery voltage, a 6-segment state-of-charge page, and grid power — pushed over MQTT and cached so it survives the radio’s sync gaps. MQTT-native (no cloud, no bridge daemon); hardware-verified on all three boards. Issues #10/#16/#17.
  • PLUGIN FRAMEWORKModes as plugins in an enum-delegation registry — LANDED and running on the build-45 fleet: an About screen, a scrolling menu and Bench mesh-view pages; gated clean (byte-exact ELF, six screens verbatim).
  • NODE MANAGERSet each board’s default screen from Home Assistant. The HA panel is DEPLOYED — it publishes the choice as retained MQTT config; the firmware half that makes boards act on it is the next wave. (HA side shipped; firmware consume in progress.) Issue #21.
  • NO-BURST ROAMING MESHToday a board goes briefly mesh-deaf while it syncs over WiFi. This retires that — keep the radio awake so the mesh never drops during a sync. A hardware soak PROVED zero mesh loss across dozens of sync windows; the roaming half (following the network as a board moves) plus full hardware-verify are IN PROGRESS. Compile-green, in review. Issue #23.
  • OTA UPDATESOver-the-air firmware over a WiFi burst, dual-slot with rollback — and each build wears a sigil VERSION name (the splash is live on glass, e.g. build 45 “Oxidized Die”). IN PROGRESS: the toolchain is proven and the update path fully designed, but rollback-on-bad-boot isn’t hardware-proven yet — so it rolls out one board at a time (canary), never fleet-wide. Not yet shipped. Issue #6.
  • BLE PRESENCEAdvertise a beacon so each board shows up as room-level presence in Home Assistant (via external anchors). Researched; advertise-only is the go, full scanning ruled out on a single radio. Issue #22.
  • COLOR TFTAdd an ST7789 and NES/GB emulation becomes possible.
fields.research.intro: A fleet of agents scouted the ESP32-C3 landscape while smol came together. The full reports live in the repo — here's the map.
fields.board.intro: Projects that run on this exact ESP32-C3 + 0.42″ OLED (72×40). A couple need their art re-drawn for the tiny screen — noted inline.
fields.board.list:
• ATOMIC14 · SINGLE-BUTTON GAMES5 games (Breakout, Micro Racer, Defender, Jump Run, Heli Cave) built for this exact board — flashing onto smol now. ↗
  • PEFF74 · ESP32-C3_OLEDThe de-facto reference sketch — correct 72×40 offset baked in. ↗
  • PHARKIE · OLED-DEMOFlash-and-go firmware.bin — 9 rotating demos, a great calibration toy. ↗
  • ALEXYERYOMIN · 72×40Board bring-up + demo code for the 72×40 panel. ↗
  • GRRTZM · MICROPYTHONMicroPython driver for this exact board — the Python route. ↗
  • ZEPHYR · esp32c3_042_oledYour board is an in-tree Zephyr RTOS target. ↗
  • NERDMINER_V2Lottery-BTC stat toy — sold on this exact hardware (mainline via forks). ↗
  • LIJIACHANG · RUSTRust (esp-hal) WiFi crypto-price ticker on this exact board — proof Rust drives it. ↗
  • ZHUHAI-ESP · ABROBOT★37 — Arduino examples (CDC, clock, OLED driver) for the 0.42″ board. ↗
  • SIGMDEL · SUPERMINI SKETCHES★112 — broad SuperMini collection incl. a 0.42″ OLED sketch. ↗
  • JULIOFORMIGA · ZEPHYR+LVGLToF distance meter on the OLED via Zephyr RTOS + LVGL. ↗
  • ESP32HOME · oled_042Clean "hello world" — ESP-IDF + PlatformIO + serial variants. ↗
  • KARAMO · MICROPYTHONCustom SSD1306.py driver + text-render demo (ABrobot). ↗
  • LUDWICH66 · PLANT SENSORSoil-moisture → MQTT/Home Assistant, µPython 72×40 driver. ↗
  • SARAH-C · BAT DETECTORUltrasonic bat-detector frequency meter on the 0.42″ OLED. ↗
  • SIGMDEL · I²C+SPIRuns the OLED (I²C) + an SPI device together; ships board defs. ↗
  • ◇ FULL LISTAll discovered repos, updated as the search agents report → docs/board-repos.md ↗
fields.case.intro: Exact-fit 3D-printable cases for the 0.42″ OLED SuperMini. No dedicated handheld shell exists yet — so for a pocket player, start from an editable one and add a button perch at GPIO9. Board is ~24.8×20.45 mm, USB-C on the short edge opposite the OLED. Full ranked list in docs/cases.md.
fields.case.list:
• TENGROM · PRINTABLESClean 2-part snap case with OLED window. Free STL, the most-referenced exact match. ↗
  • DB59 · MAKERWORLDBest-reviewed exact fit — with/without a wiring window. Free STL/3MF. ↗
  • MAKERWORLD 2264801Snap case, OLED + USB-C access, no supports. Finished-product look, free. ↗
  • CULTS3D · PRINT-IN-PLACEExposes BOOT/RESET + LEDs + OLED — closest to game-ready. Paid. ↗
  • GRABCAD · EDITABLE CADSource CAD — the best base to widen the window + add a button for a handheld. ↗
  • JCVG · PARAMETRIC (bare)OpenSCAD parametric SuperMini case — regenerate with an OLED window + button. ↗
fields.influences.intro: smol didn't appear from nowhere. Its ESP-NOW → MQTT → Home Assistant plumbing is commodity — a well-trodden pattern many have built. What's actually novel is the whole: a no_std Rust game-console mesh on $3 boards, a deliberate single-radio burst time-share, and a retained-payload → mesh-rebroadcast downlink to display-only leaves. Below are the projects that shaped it, grouped by the thread each runs in — credited honestly, with what smol borrows and where it diverges. A few handed over portable ideas; most gave lineage, identity and nerve rather than code.
fields.influences.list:
◇ Mesh & routing
  • MESHTASTICThe mesh ethos smol admires — multi-hop, self-healing, friendly per-node names, an MQTT bridge to the world. smol's realm-sigil board names and its #13/#14 roadmap trace straight back here; different PHY — ESP-NOW on 2.4 GHz, not LoRa. ↗
  • PAINLESSMESHA self-organizing WiFi mesh where every node is both STA and AP. The road smol didn't take — it chose ESP-NOW for lower power, lower latency and tighter game timing. The honest counterfactual. ↗
  • ALTHEAAn OpenWrt-based decentralized, incentivized mesh ISP — self-organizing routed mesh, per-link WireGuard tunnels, Babel routing, IPv6, bandwidth settled by micropayments on its own L1 chain. smol borrows the self-organizing-mesh + per-link-encryption ethos (its lightweight analog is ESP-NOW LMK keys, #12) and the routed-multi-hop idea (Babel is prior art for #13); it leaves behind the blockchain settlement and full IPv6-over-mesh — node-ids are smol's lightweight addressing. The most different influence — an L3 incentivized ISP vs smol's L2 game mesh — architectural inspiration, not a template. ↗
  • ZHNETWORK · aZHOLTIKOVA routed, multi-hop ESP-NOW mesh library that bridges to MQTT + Home Assistant autodiscovery. Direct prior art for true mesh routing — and ahead of smol here: smol does single-hop relay today and lists routed multi-hop as #13 roadmap, not shipped. ↗
  • ESP-NOW → MQTT → HA · LATTICThe exact ESP-NOW → MQTT → Home Assistant gateway pattern smol independently arrived at — direct prior art for smol's relay bridge, credited plainly. ↗
◇ Local-first ESP ↔ HA
  • BATTERY-EMULATORESP32 firmware (LilyGo, C++) that emulates a BMS to bridge repurposed EV battery packs to home solar inverters over CAN bus, with its own web UI. The one influence smol is actually wired to — smol reads its battery telemetry over MQTT and mirrors it on the OLED, a live interop neighbor, not just an idea. It bridges CAN↔inverter on the mains side; smol is a battery-powered ESP-NOW mesh display/relay — different job, same MQTT-native, local-first, HA-first philosophy. ↗
  • TASMOTAOpen-source, MQTT-first, local-first alternative firmware for ESP smart-home devices — a rich cmnd/stat/tele topic scheme, runtime config over MQTT (no reflash), an on-device web UI, a rules engine, robust OTA, HA MQTT discovery. smol's closest genre-cousin: it borrows the runtime-config-over-MQTT / no-reflash ethos (exactly what the node manager does), the MQTT topic conventions, and the OTA + HA-discovery + local-first values (smol just firewalled off a cloud-phoning logger). Differs in form — Tasmota is C++/Arduino on always-on-WiFi mains devices with a web UI; smol is no_std Rust, burst/battery, single-radio, so the control surface relocates to HA. Prior art for local-MQTT-ESP→HA that smol independently lands in a game-console/mesh form. ↗
  • WLEDThe gold standard for Home Assistant polish — device-grouped typed entities, auto-discovery, its own ESP-NOW sync, a slick on-device web UI. smol borrows the entity-structure lesson; it can't host an on-device UI (the burst radio is deaf most of the time), so its node manager lives in HA instead. ↗
  • ESPHOMEThe YAML→firmware ecosystem and its always-connected native API. smol stays MQTT-native on purpose — the persistent native API fights the single-radio burst model, and no no_std-Rust ESPHome exists to build on anyway. ↗
  • ESP_WIFI_REPEATER · MARTIN-GERProof that an ESP is a real router — concurrent STA+AP, NAT, a console, MQTT, ACLs. Inspires smol's gateway-as-network-citizen role and shows concurrent multi-role radio is achievable. ↗
  • ESP3DESP8266/ESP32 firmware that bridges a 3D-printer or CNC serial port onto WiFi with a web UI — serial-over-WiFi, telnet, even Home Assistant notifications. smol borrows the ESP-as-WiFi-bridge-with-web-control appliance ethos: make a dumb serial device networked. Differs — ESP3D is one device ↔ one browser; smol is a multi-node mesh whose UI is the OLED + HA, not a per-node web page. ↗
  • ESP-LINK · JEELABSAn ESP8266 WiFi↔serial bridge that can remotely reflash an attached AVR/ARM MCU over WiFi (an stk500v1 programmer fed by HTTP hex upload), with a web console. smol borrows the gateway-bridge ethos and cites it as prior art for firmware-over-WiFi alongside OTA (#6). The honest distinction: esp-link flashes a separate attached chip — it's the programmer, not the target — while smol's OTA self-updates the ESP itself into an inactive flash slot. Same spirit, different mechanism. ↗
◇ Cheap-ESP audacity — smol's soul
  • ESP32 MARAUDERA WiFi/BLE multi-tool on a cheap ESP32 with a tiny touchscreen and a menu-driven handheld UI. smol borrows the form-factor genre — cheap ESP + tiny screen + menu navigation is exactly smol's physical shape and UX — plus a radio-manipulation kinship (both bend the 2.4 GHz radio to their will). Differs — smol is a cooperative mesh node, not a pentest tool, and it manipulates its own radio for ESP-NOW coexistence, not other people's networks. ↗
  • LEAFMINERA FOSS Bitcoin solo-miner for the ESP8266/ESP32 (NerdMiner-family, built on NerdSHA256plus) — openly a learning toy, since actually mining a block is astronomically unlikely. smol borrows the soul: a $5 chip doing something gloriously improbable for the joy and the learning of it — the same spirit as a shared-world snake MMO running on a clock. Differs — smol is a useful mesh (telemetry, time, display), not a lottery ticket. ↗
  • ATOMIC14Five single-button games (Breakout, Micro Racer, Defender, Jump Run, Heli Cave) built for this exact ESP32-C3 + 0.42″ OLED — and now flashed onto smol. The closest thing to a direct ancestor: living proof this precise board is a game console. Squarely in smol's play lineage. ↗
◇ Remote-management UX
  • PIKVMAn open-source IP-KVM on a Raspberry Pi — a pure-HTML5 web UI for remote keyboard/video/mouse, power control and BIOS access, with no client install. smol borrows the remote-management-appliance ethos and its clean web-control UX: manage the physical thing from anywhere, through a browser. Aspirational for smol's HA-side control surface and the future #21 node manager. Differs — PiKVM is a Linux Pi driving one other computer; smol is a fleet of bare-metal ESP32 mesh nodes managed via HA/MQTT + an OLED. ↗
◇ Lean real-time embedded
  • GRBLReal-time CNC motion-control firmware in tightly optimized C for an Arduino-class AVR — a G-code planner that looks 16 moves ahead, emits up to 30 kHz of jitter-free step pulses, and honors real-time override commands mid-stream. smol borrows the lean-real-time-firmware craft — deterministic behavior squeezed from a tiny MCU — and a loose design echo: GRBL interleaves out-of-band real-time commands with a streamed buffer, roughly the shape of smol draining ESP-NOW RX between MQTT poll cycles. Differs — GRBL is single-purpose motion with no radio or mesh; the echo is lineage, not a code steal. ↗

◇ BLE presence (the #22 basis) — smol's beacon-tracking idea would build on Bermuda (HACS trilateration that reuses existing bluetooth_proxy anchors) and ESPresense (dedicated MQTT anchors). Prior art and reference, not code smol borrows.
fields.footer.note: smol — built on a fingertip, documented on a page that edits itself.
